items.title: NITI Aayog Gets Two New Full-Time Members

items.title: Nine-Year-Old Mountaineer from Siddipet Scales Australia's Highest Peak
items.whyInNews: A nine-year-old mountaineer from Siddipet has scaled Australia's highest peak, Mount Kosciuszko, on May 2, 2026.
items.summary: The nine-year-old mountaineer, Vihaan, has achieved a remarkable feat by scaling Australia's highest peak, Mount Kosciuszko. Vihaan undertook the expedition with a social message, carrying a banner urging youth to 'Say No to Drugs'. The achievement is a testament to Vihaan's determination and courage, and is expected to inspire other young people to take up mountaineering.

items.title: Access to Generic Risdiplam Sought for People with Spinal Muscular Atrophy
items.whyInNews: Patients with Spinal Muscular Atrophy (SMA) have sought access to generic Risdiplam, a life-saving drug, on May 1, 2026.
items.summary: Patients with Spinal Muscular Atrophy (SMA) have sought access to generic Risdiplam, a life-saving drug. The drug is currently not affordable for many patients, and the patients have appealed to the Prime Minister to make it available at a lower cost. The appeal is significant, as it highlights the need for affordable healthcare for people with rare diseases.
